metadata.seed: My company has a holiday party and I just can't... go... I don't want to socialize and parties annoy me to no end. All the small talk, all the people... I don't want to go. At times I feel like I'm allergic to people.

patient.query: My company has a holiday party and I just can't... go... I don't want to socialize and parties annoy me to no end. All the small talk, all the people... I don't want to go. At times I feel like I'm allergic to people.

llm_response.response: It's completely understandable that you might be feeling this way about the holiday party. Social events can indeed be overwhelming at times, and it's important to prioritize your comfort and well-being. If you don't feel up to attending, it's perfectly okay to decline the invitation. You could express your feelings honestly, letting them know that you value the opportunity to connect with everyone but need some time for self-care during this busy season. It might also be helpful to suggest alternative ways to engage if possible, such as a virtual gathering or a one-on-one catch-up with a colleague. Remember, it's okay to set boundaries and prioritize your mental health.
